entry.title: Senate moves one step closer to passing health care cyber reforms
entry.content:
A legislative package that would overhaul cybersecurity practices at the Department of Health and Human Services sailed through committee. The post Senate moves one step closer to passing health care cyber reforms appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: How ‘silent probing’ can make your security playbook a liability
entry.content:
Silent probing uses AI to measure your team’s response patterns over time, building a behavioral profile that makes follow-on attacks harder to spot and easier to time. The post How ‘silent probing’ can make your security playbook a liability appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: The FBI’s cyber chief is using Winter SHIELD to accelerate China prep, threat intelligence sharing
entry.content:
Brett Leatherman is running the bureau's most public cyber campaign yet, pushing basic security hygiene while quietly preparing industry for stepped-up Chinese threats. The post The FBI’s cyber chief is using Winter SHIELD to accelerate China prep, threat intelligence sharing appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Google addresses actively exploited Qualcomm zero-day in fresh batch of 129 Android vulnerabilities
entry.content:
The company’s latest security update contains the highest number of Android vulnerabilities patched in a single month since April 2018. The post Google addresses actively exploited Qualcomm zero-day in fresh batch of 129 Android vulnerabilities appeared first on CyberScoop.
📰 Source: CyberScoop | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Attackers Now Need Just 29 Minutes to Own a Network
entry.content:
Credential misuse, AI tools, and security blind spots help attackers move through breached networks faster than ever, CrowdStrike finds.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: 'Richter Scale' Model Measures Magnitude of OT Cyber Incidents
entry.content:
ICS/OT experts have devised a scoring system for rating the severity and effects of cybersecurity events in operational technology environments.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: PCI Council Says Threats to Payments Systems Are Speeding Up
entry.content:
The PCI Security Standards Council experienced a record year in many regards, but its first annual report shows it needs to work even faster to stay ahead of attackers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: RAMP Forum Seizure Fractures Ransomware Ecosystem
entry.content:
Researchers suggest defenders monitor how these malicious groups re-form and leverage the useful threat intel to guide their next moves.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cisco SD-WAN Zero-Day Under Exploitation for 3 Years
entry.content:
The maximum-severity vulnerability CVE-2026-20127 was exploited by an unknown but sophisticated threat actor who left very little evidence behind.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Marquis v. SonicWall Lawsuit Ups the Breach Blame Game
entry.content:
When a company gets breached through a third-party security vendor, who should bear responsibility? For one FinTech company, the answer is the firewall provider.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Claude Code Security Shows Promise, Not Perfection
entry.content:
Claude Code's introduction rippled across the stock market, but researchers and analysts say its impact was overstated, as they peel back the layers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: The Case for Why Better Breach Transparency Matters
entry.content:
It's become a standard practice for organizations to disclose the bare minimum about a data breach, or worse — not disclose the incident at all.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Life Mirrors Art: Ransomware Hits Hospitals on TV & IRL
entry.content:
HBO's "The Pitt" is showing audiences what a real Mississippi healthcare system is going through this week, thanks to a ransomware attack.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Critical OpenClaw Vulnerability Exposes AI Agent Risks
entry.content:
The now-patched flaw is the latest in a growing string of security issues associated with the viral AI tool, which has seen rapid adoption among developers.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Canadian Tire Data Breach Impacts 38 Million Accounts
entry.content:
Names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, and encrypted passwords were compromised in the attack. The post Canadian Tire Data Breach Impacts 38 Million Accounts appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Hackers Weaponize Claude Code in Mexican Government Cyberattack
entry.content:
The AI was abused to write exploits, create tools, and automatically exfiltrate over 150GB of data. The post Hackers Weaponize Claude Code in Mexican Government Cyberattack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: US-Israel and Iran Trade Cyberattacks: Pro-West Hacks Cause Disruption as Tehran Retaliates
entry.content:
Both sides conduct hacking and other attacks, including the deployment of wiper malware, DDoS, and disruptions to critical infrastructure. The post US-Israel and Iran Trade Cyberattacks: Pro-West Hacks Cause Disruption as Tehran Retaliates appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: AWS Expands Security Hub Into a Cross-Domain Security Platform
entry.content:
The AWS Security Hub Extended plan aims to reduce security tool sprawl by correlating findings across multiple security domains. The post AWS Expands Security Hub Into a Cross-Domain Security Platform appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Madison Square Garden Data Breach Confirmed Months After Hacker Attack
entry.content:
The company is one of the many victims of the 2025 Oracle E-Business Suite (EBS) hacking campaign. The post Madison Square Garden Data Breach Confirmed Months After Hacker Attack appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: OpenClaw Vulnerability Allowed Websites to Hijack AI Agents
entry.content:
Malicious websites could open a WebSocket connection to localhost on the OpenClaw gateway port, brute force passwords, and take control of the agent. The post OpenClaw Vulnerability Allowed Websites to Hijack AI Agents appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Vulnerability Allowed Hijacking Chrome’s Gemini Live AI Assistant
entry.content:
Malicious extensions could hijack the Gemini Live in Chrome feature to spy on users and steal their files. The post Vulnerability Allowed Hijacking Chrome’s Gemini Live AI Assistant appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: British organizations urged to be alert to threat of Iranian cyberattacks
entry.content:
In an advisory, the U.K.'s National Cyber Security Centre warned British organizations to review and strengthen their cyber defenses amid the evolving situation in Iran and throughout the Middle East.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: University of Hawaiʻi Cancer Center confirms data leak following ransomware attack
entry.content:
Part of the breach was traced back to a Multiethnic Cohort (MEC) Study established in 1993, which used driver’s license numbers and voter registration records to recruit participants.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Cyber Command disrupted Iranian comms, sensors, top general says
entry.content:
U.S. Cyber Command conducted online attacks against Iranian communications systems that the country’s top general said set the stage for the joint bombing campaign with Israel.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: ‘Starkiller’ Phishing Service Proxies Real Login Pages, MFA
entry.content:
Most phishing websites are little more than static copies of login pages for popular online destinations, and they are often quickly taken down by anti-abuse activists and security firms. But a stealthy new phishing-as-a-service offering lets customers sidestep both of these pitfalls: It uses cleverly disguised links to load the target brand's real website, and then acts as a relay between the target and the legitimate site -- forwarding the victim's username, password and multi-factor authentic
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

entry.title: Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026
entry.content:
The HIPAA Journal has compiled healthcare data breach statistics from October 2009, when the Department of Health and Human Services […] The post Healthcare Data Breach Statistics – Updated for 2026 appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Center for Advanced Eye Care; Southwest C.A.R.E Center; Evergreen Healthcare Group Announce Data Breaches
entry.content:
The Center for Advanced Eye Care in Pennsylvania/Delaware, Southwest C.A.R.E Center in New Mexico, and Evergreen Healthcare Group in Washington […] The post Center for Advanced Eye Care; Southwest C.A.R.E Center; Evergreen Healthcare Group Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Carolina Foot & Ankle Associates Notifies Patients About December 2025 Cyberattack
entry.content:
Cyberattacks and data breaches have been announced by the healthcare providers Carolina Foot & Ankle Associates, New Age Dermatology, and […] The post Carolina Foot & Ankle Associates Notifies Patients About December 2025 Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Rebound Orthopedics & Neurosurgery Pays $2.5 Million to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit
entry.content:
Rebound Orthopedics & Neurosurgery, a Vancouver, WA-based orthopedic and neurosurgery practice, has agreed to pay $2,500,000 to settle a class action […] The post Rebound Orthopedics & Neurosurgery Pays $2.5 Million to Settle Data Breach Lawsuit appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Soaring Insider Breach Costs Driven by Shadow AI Use
entry.content:
On average, businesses with 500 or more employees are losing an average of $19.5 million a year due to insider […] The post Soaring Insider Breach Costs Driven by Shadow AI Use appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Apex Spine & Neurosurgery & North Central Behavioral Health Systems Announce Data Breaches
entry.content:
Data breaches have been announced by Apex Spine & Neurosurgery in Georgia and North Central Behavioral Health Systems in Illinois. […] The post Apex Spine & Neurosurgery & North Central Behavioral Health Systems Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: January 2026 Healthcare Data Breach Report
entry.content:
The HHS’ Office for Civil Rights (OCR) healthcare data breach portal shows a slight month-over-month decline in large healthcare data […] The post January 2026 Healthcare Data Breach Report appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Asheville Eye Associates Settles Lawsuit Stemming from DragonForce Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
Asheville Eye Associates, an eye care provider serving patients in Western North Carolina, has agreed to settle class action litigation […] The post Asheville Eye Associates Settles Lawsuit Stemming from DragonForce Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: University of Hawai’i Cancer Center: 1.24 Million Individuals Affected by 2025 Ransomware Attack
entry.content:
The University of Hawai’i Cancer Center (UHCC) has confirmed that up to 1.24 million individuals may have been affected by […] The post University of Hawai’i Cancer Center: 1.24 Million Individuals Affected by 2025 Ransomware Attack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.title: Court Refuses to Slice Up CiCi’s Cyber Extortion Coverage
entry.content:
Andrea DeField and S. Alice Weeks of Hunton Andrews Kurth write: In the rarely litigated space of cyber insurance, the Northern District of Texas issued a win for cyber policyholders this week, offering a clear reminder to insurers that if they want to restrict coverage, they must draft the policy to clearly do so. In CiCi... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Connecticut Senate Bill Raises the Stakes on Data Breach Response
entry.content:
Hayley Steele and Gregory Szewczyk of Ballard Spahr write: A new bill introduced in Connecticut—Connecticut Senate Bill 117, An Act Concerning Breaches of Security Involving Electronic Personal Information—would create mandatory forensic examination requirements for entities that experience a “massive breach of security,” defined as a data breach affecting at least 100,000 Connecticut residents, and imposes... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: South Korean Police Lose Seized Crypto By Posting Password Online
entry.content:
Kyle Torpey reports: South Korea’s National Tax Service seized crypto assets during recent enforcement actions against 124 high-value tax evaders, but now, a large chunk of that crypto cash has been lost. The operation originally resulted in the confiscation of crypto holdings worth about 8.1 billion won, or roughly $5.6 million. However, officials later issued... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: CISA Releases New Guidance on Assembling Multi-Disciplinary Insider Threat Management Teams
entry.content:
Ashley Nyquist, Ashden Fein, Caleb Skeath, John Webster Leslie, Matthew Harden, Catherine McGrath, and Samar Amidi of Covington and Burling write: On January 28, 2026, the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) published a new resource on Assembling a Multi-Disciplinary Insider Threat Management Team.The guidance is intended to assist critical infrastructure stakeholders, which includes private... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Shutdown Stalls Compliance Plans for Cyber Breach Reporting Rule
entry.content:
Cassandre Coyer reports: A partial government shutdown threatens to further derail a key federal cybersecurity agency’s incident reporting rule—and delay answers that companies need to comply. The Department of Homeland Security shutdown, now entering its third week, may push back the finish line for a Biden-era rule that would create stringent disclosure requirements for critical infrastructure entities after... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Hacktivists claim to have hacked Homeland Security to release ICE contract data
entry.content:
Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai reports: A group of hacktivists calling themselves “Department of Peace” claimed to have hacked the Department of Homeland Security (DHS), leaking allegedly stolen documents online. On Sunday, the nonprofit transparency collective DDoSecrets published data relating to contracts between DHS, Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), and more than 6,000 companies, including defense contractors Anduril, L3Harris, Raytheon,... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Project Compass: first operational results against The Com network
entry.content:
Europol reports: In its first year of operation, Project Compass has delivered concrete operational results against “The Com”, a decentralised extremist network targeting minors and vulnerable individuals both online and offline. Since January 2025, Project Compass has contributed to: 4 victims safeguarded 30 perpetrators arrested 62 identified and partially identified victims 179 identified and partially... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Wisconsin k-12 district hit by weeklong outage
entry.content:
Dysruption Hub reports: A reported “cyber incident” left the Denmark School District in the Village of Denmark, Wisconsin, without internet access for five school days, forcing teachers and students to rely on paper-based workarounds, according to a local news report. But that “cyberincident” appears to be a cyberattack by INC Ransom, or so the gang... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Data from Insight Hospital and Medical Center Leaked on Dark Web
entry.content:
On or about January 26, 2026, Insight Hospital and Medical Center (“Insight”) in Chicago issued a substitute notice. It states that in September 2025, Insight learned of unusual activity within its network. An investigation subsequently determined that an unauthorized individual accessed the network between August 22, 2025 and September 11, 2025. As of the date... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Evoke Wellness at Hilliard updates its breach notification
entry.content:
On February 27, external counsel for OCAT, LLC dba Evoke Wellness at Hilliard (“Evoke”), submitted a breach notification to the Maine Attorney General’s Office. The sample notification letter submitted with it claims that the Ohio addiction treatment center learned of an incident on August 7, 2025: On August 7, 2025, OCAT became aware of unauthorized... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

entry.title: Europol-led crackdown on The Com hackers leads to 30 arrests
entry.content:
A yearlong Europol-coordinated operation dubbed "Project Compass" has led to 30 arrests and 179 suspects being tied to "The Com," an online cybercrime collective that targets children and teenagers. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: APT37 hackers use new malware to breach air-gapped networks
entry.content:
North Korean hackers are deploying newly uncovered tools to move data between internet-connected and air-gapped systems, spread via removable drives, and conduct covert surveillance. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: QuickLens Chrome extension steals crypto, shows ClickFix attack
entry.content:
A Chrome extension named "QuickLens - Search Screen with Google Lens" has been removed from the Chrome Web Store after it was compromised to push malware and attempt to steal crypto from thousands of users. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: ClawJacked attack let malicious websites hijack OpenClaw to steal data
entry.content:
Security researchers have disclosed a high-severity vulnerability dubbed "ClawJacked" in the popular AI agent OpenClaw that allowed a malicious website to silently bruteforce access to a locally running instance and take control over it. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: How Deepfakes and Injection Attacks Are Breaking Identity Verification
entry.content:
Deepfakes and injection attacks are targeting identity verification moments, from onboarding to account recovery. Incode explains why enterprises must validate the full session—media, device integrity, and behavior—to stop synthetic and injected attacks in real time. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: UK warns of Iranian cyberattack risks amid Middle-East conflict
entry.content:
The United Kingdom's National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) alerted British organizations to a heightened risk of Iranian cyberattacks amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Alabama man pleads guilty to hacking, extorting hundreds of women
entry.content:
A 22-year-old Alabama man pleaded guilty to extortion, cyberstalking, and computer fraud charges after hijacking the social media accounts of hundreds of young women (including minors). [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: CyberStrikeAI tool adopted by hackers for AI-powered attacks
entry.content:
Researchers warn that a newly identified open-source AI security testing platform called CyberStrikeAI was used by the same threat actor behind a recent campaign that breached hundreds of Fortinet FortiGate firewalls. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

entry.title: Unknown
entry.content:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

entry.content:
Denmark School District reported a ransomware. Attributed to INC_RANSOM.

entry.title: Flash Charm, Inc. d/b/a Idera
entry.content:
